channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump’s remarks on Iran raise grave concerns about international law and US leadership
item.description: The article discusses a deeply troubling statement by US President Donald Trump, in which he declared that a “whole civilisation will die tonight” in reference to Iran. This rhetoric, reminiscent of a...

item.title: Tensions Rise: US-Iran Conflict and Cinematic Inspirations
item.description: The ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran continue to escalate, prompting comparisons to potential Hollywood blockbusters focused on daring rescue operations. While a real-life drama unf...

item.title: Severe thunderstorms and heatwave expected across central and southern South Africa
item.description: South Africa faces a turbulent weather day on Thursday, with a mix of thunderstorms, high temperatures, and damaging winds affecting several regions. The central interior, particularly parts of the No...

item.title: Pakistan Receives Strong Remittance Inflows Despite Middle East Conflict
item.description: Despite the ongoing war in the Middle East, Pakistan witnessed a significant rise in workers’ remittances during March 2026, with inflows reaching USD 3.8 billion — a 17 per cent increase from Februar...

item.title: FBI Dancer Ezra Starts Youth Campaign Supporting President Ruto Amid Young Voter Divisions
item.description: Famous FBI dancer Ezra has started a campaign called 'Vijana Wa Ruto' to gather support for President William Ruto ahead of the 2027 general elections. This comes amid increasing political activity am...

item.title: Most missing Pakistanis in Italy boat tragedy had travelled under false pretences
item.description: GUJRAT: In a tragic incident off the coast of Italy, a boat carrying over 100 illegal migrants capsized, leaving many Pakistanis missing. Well-placed sources have informed that most of these Pakistani...

item.title: Nepra approves Rs1.42 per unit fuel cost adjustment for April bills
item.description: The National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Nepra) has approved an additional fuel cost adjustment (FCA) of Rs1.42 per unit for electricity consumed in February 2026, which will be reflected in ...

item.title: Recapitalisation of Nigerian Insurance Sector Should Boost Strength, Not Limit Growth
item.description: Di ongoing recapitalisation wey dey happen because of di Nigerian Insurance Industry Reform Act (NIIRA) don start to change how insurance sector dey structured. Some insurance companies don reach di n...

item.title: Fire Damage at Riverlea Secondary School Estimated at R45 Million, Repairs to Begin Soon
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Education faces new infrastructure challenges after another fire ravaged Riverlea Secondary School in Johannesburg earlier this week. The blaze destroyed three classrooms, a ...

item.title: North Korea Tests Advanced Cluster-Bomb Missile and Electronic Warfare Systems
item.description: North Korea has conducted a series of military tests showcasing its latest conventional weapons, including a cluster-bomb-tipped ballistic missile and an electromagnetic weapon system. The tests, over...

item.title: Residents battle noise and legal loopholes in Melbourne strata dispute
item.description: A long-running dispute in Melbourne’s Malvern East has highlighted serious flaws in Victoria’s strata laws. Fiona McAllister and her husband, Nick McCaffrey, say their lives have been upended by persi...

item.title: India Withdraws from Hosting 2028 UN Climate Summit
item.description: India has officially decided not to host the United Nations' annual climate Conference of the Parties (COP33) in 2028. This decision was communicated earlier this month, but the reasons behind the wit...

item.title: Mining juniors Element 25 and BluGlass present to fund managers seeking investment
item.description: On Thursday, a number of listed mining junior companies, including Element 25 and BluGlass, presented to fund managers as part of ongoing efforts to raise capital. These sessions, commonly known in th...

item.title: How Parents Can Manage Children’s Screen Time During Holidays
item.description: With the April school holidays here, many children are spending more hours at home, which naturally means more time in front of screens—be it television, social media, or streaming platforms. While di...

item.title: Carney’s Strategy for a Majority and Gladu’s Stance on Party Switchers
item.description: On April 8, 2026, Canadian political news focused on key developments involving Finance Minister Carney and former Conservative MP Marilyn Gladu. John Ivison commented on Carney's efforts to consolida...

item.title: India's Domestic Workers Need Legal Recognition and Protection Beyond Labour Codes
item.description: The article discusses the urgent need for India to enact a separate law safeguarding the rights of domestic workers, despite the government’s reliance on its newly consolidated labour codes. Domestic ...

item.title: Lookman helps Atlético beat Barcelona, PSG defeat Liverpool in UCL
item.description: On Wednesday, April 9, 2026, Atlético Madrid stunned Barcelona with a 2–0 victory at Camp Nou, securing their first win there since 2006. The match saw Ademola Lookman, who joined from Atalanta in Jan...

item.title: NSCDC uncovers illegal crude oil storage site in Ikwerre, Rivers State
item.description: The Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC), Rivers State Command, don find one big illegal dump site for Igwuruta, Ikwerre LGA. The site na believed to dey used for store stolen crude oil. A...

item.title: Iran warns of severe retaliation as Israeli strikes kill hundreds in Lebanon amid fragile Iran–US ceasefire
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have warned that they will deliver a “regret-inducing” and “heavy” response if Israeli attacks on Lebanon do not stop at once. Their statement, carried by Iranian state med...

item.title: Cape Town banker faces third bail bid over major cocaine and firearms case
item.description: Raed Cupido, known in media reports as the 'cocaine banker', has appeared in court for his third attempt to secure bail. He is awaiting trial on serious drug and firearm charges following his arrest i...
